seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with LangChain enables sophisticated conversational agents using chains, agents, and RAG pipelines. However, these systems are prone to hallucinations, tool call loops, context overflow, and inconsistent formatting that can degrade user experience and trust. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response accuracy, coherence, and safety in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangChain AI chatbot architecture starts with intent classification using a lightweight LLM chain to route user queries. For knowledge-based questions, it triggers a RAG pipeline that retrieves relevant documents from a vector store like FAISS or Pinecone, augments the prompt, and generates responses. The conversational agent maintains multi-turn memory via ConversationBufferWindowMemory or custom token-truncated buffers, while tool-calling agents handle actions like database queries or API calls. Safety layers filter toxic content and PII before final output.

The flow processes user input through a router chain, selects between RAG retrieval, tool execution, or direct generation, maintains conversation history within token limits, and applies output parsers for structured responses. This modular design scales well but introduces failure points at each step.

Vex integration wraps critical invoke() calls to verify outputs against accuracy, coherence, and safety policies before reaching users.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Agent generates plausible but incorrect facts when retrieved context is irrelevant or insufficient
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks against ground truth documents and hallucination detection models

risks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
risks.description: Agent repeatedly calls the same tool without progress, consuming tokens and causing infinite loops
risks.mitigation: Vex loop detection via state tracking and maximum iteration enforcement

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Conversation history exceeds LLM token limits, causing truncation and context loss
risks.mitigation: Vex token counting and dynamic summarization before chain invocation

risks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
risks.description: Different chain components produce varying JSON schemas or text formats, breaking parsers
risks.mitigation: Vex structured output validation with JSON schema enforcement

implementation.title: RAG Retrieval Chain Setup
implementation.description: Basic RAG pipeline with FAISS vector store and conversational memory
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferWindowMemory
from langchain.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain.embeddings import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain.llms import OpenAI

# Setup vector store with documents
embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()
db = FAISS.from_texts(["Your knowledge documents"], embeddings)

# Conversational memory
memory = ConversationBufferWindowMemory(k=5, return_messages=True)

# RAG chain
llm = OpenAI(temperature=0)
qa_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(
    llm=llm,
    chain_type="stuff",
    retriever=db.as_retriever(),
    memory=memory
)

# Invoke with user query
result = qa_chain.invoke({"query": "What is the capital of France?"})

implementation.title: Conversational Agent with Tools
implementation.description: Agent with search tool and memory for multi-turn conversations
implementation.code:
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType
from langchain.tools import DuckDuckGoSearchRun
from langchain.memory import ConversationBufferMemory
from langchain.llms import OpenAI

llm = OpenAI(temperature=0)
search = DuckDuckGoSearchRun()
tools = [search]

memory = ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key="chat_history")
agent = initialize_agent(
    tools,
    llm,
    agent=AgentType.CONVERSATIONAL_REACT_DESCRIPTION,
    memory=memory,
    verbose=True
)

response = agent.run("What's the weather like today?")

implementation.title: TypeScript LangChain Chatbot Chain
implementation.description: TypeScript implementation using LangChain.js with OpenAI

implementation.title: Intent Router Chain
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains.router import MultiPromptChain
from langchain.chains.llm import LLMChain
from langchain.prompts import PromptTemplate
from langchain.llms import OpenAI

llm = OpenAI(temperature=0)

rag_prompt = PromptTemplate.from_template("Answer using context: {context}\nQuestion: {input}")
rag_chain = LLMChain(llm=llm, prompt=rag_prompt)

general_prompt = PromptTemplate.from_template("{input}")
general_chain = LLMChain(llm=llm, prompt=general_prompt)

router_chain = MultiPromptChain.from_llmchains([
    rag_chain,
    general_chain
], llm, "")

result = router_chain.invoke({"input": "What is LangChain?"})

vexIntegration.title: Wrap RAG Chain with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Verify hallucination and accuracy in retrieval responses
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@vex.verify(
    checks=[
        "response_accuracy",
        "hallucination_detection",
        "safety_filtering"
    ],
    risk_profile="high"
)
def verified_rag_query(query: str):
    result = qa_chain.invoke({"query": query})
    return result["result"]

# Usage
safe_response = verified_rag_query("What is the capital of France?")
print(safe_response)
